steps.title: Use the roots shortcut
steps.content: $f(x)=(x-2)(x+15)$ is an upward-opening parabola. Its roots are $x=2$ and $x=-15$.

steps.title: Find the midpoint of the roots
steps.content:
The vertex of a parabola in factored form lies halfway between the two x-intercepts:
$$x=\frac{2+(-15)}{2}=\frac{-13}{2}$$

steps.title: Answer
steps.content:
The function reaches its minimum when $x=-\frac{13}{2}$.

The question asks for the input where the minimum occurs, not the minimum output value, so the x-coordinate of the vertex is the final answer.
